---
title: "DESeq2_Matrin3"
date: "`r format(Sys.Date(), '%Y-%m-%d')`"
author:
  - name: "Kim Nguyen"
format:
  html:
    code-fold: TRUE
    code-overflow: scroll
    code-summary: "Show code"
    code-tools: true
    code-line-numbers: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 5
    toc-location: right
    toc-expand: true
    number-sections: true
    theme: sandstone
    fontsize: 11pt
    linestretch: 1.5
    fig-format: png
    cap-location: margin
    body-width: 900px
    sidebar-width: 300px
    page-layout: full
    crossref:
      fig-title: Fig
    embed-resources: true
    link-external-newwindow: true
    smooth-scroll: true
    css: css.css
    execute:
      echo: true
      warning: false
---

Differential Gene Expression (DGE): This refers to changes in the total abundance of all mRNA transcripts produced from a single gene between different conditions or cell types.
Differential Alternative Splicing (DAS): This refers to changes in the relative proportions of different transcript isoforms produced from the same gene, while the total amount of mRNA for that gene may remain unchanged. 

* Library
```{r message = FALSE, warnings=FALSE}
# For Plotting & Visualization
#install.packages("VennDiagram")
#BiocManager::install("genefilter")
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyverse)
library(plotly)
library(RColorBrewer)
library(ggrepel)
library(markdown)
library(ggrastr)
library(ggpointdensity)
library(magrittr)
library(dplyr)
library(gridExtra)
library(magrittr)
library(magick)
library(RColorBrewer)
library(colorRamp2)
library(ggvenn)
library(kableExtra)
library(ggdist)
library(ggsci)
library(ggsignif) # for geom_signif()
library(ashr)
library(ggrepel)
library(ggpp)
library(stringr)
library(forcats)
library(scales)
# For analysis
library(pheatmap)
library(ComplexHeatmap)
library(DESeq2)
library(gprofiler2)
library(clusterProfiler)
library(enrichplot)
library(showtext)# Effect size for U2AF2 pair
library(cluster)
library(factoextra)
library(genefilter)
library(ComplexUpset)
library(tidyverse)
library(ggstatsplot)
library(gProfileR)

# annotated mouse genome
library(org.Mm.eg.db)
library(AnnotationDbi)

#to combine multiple ggplot
library(patchwork)
library(reshape2)
library(ggpubr)
library(cowplot)
```

# Load outputs from previous analyses
```{r message = FALSE, warnings=FALSE}
outpath <- "/Users/tinkienoob/Master_thesis/MATR3/Deseq2/"
# Load the saved RDS file
gene_anno_df <- readRDS(file = "/Users/tinkienoob/Master_thesis/MATR3/mm39_anno_gtf.RDS")

load("/Users/tinkienoob/Master_thesis/MATR3/Deseq2/dds_df_full/fulldds_DESeq2.RData")

m3specific_site <- readRDS("/Users/tinkienoob/Master_thesis/MATR3/Hypertribe_bindingsites_analysis/m3specific_sites2.rds")
all_control_list_HT <- readRDS("/Users/tinkienoob/Master_thesis/MATR3/Hypertribe_bindingsites_analysis/HT_control_list.rds")
rank_M3tx_df <- readRDS("/Users/tinkienoob/Master_thesis/MATR3/rank_M3_target_manual.rds") %>%
  mutate(GeneID = sub("(.+)\\..*", "\\1", gene_id)) %>%
  # 🔥 ROBUST RECODING 🔥
  mutate(
    rank_char = as.character(rank), # Prevent silent factor coercion
    rank = case_when(
      grepl("Tier ?1", rank_char, ignore.case = TRUE) ~ "High",
      grepl("Tier ?2", rank_char, ignore.case = TRUE) ~ "Intermediate",
      grepl("Tier ?3", rank_char, ignore.case = TRUE) ~ "Low",
      TRUE ~ rank_char # Leaves "Others" untouched safely
    )
  )
#table(rank_M3tx_df$rank)
save_thesis_plot <- function(plot, filename,
                             width = 10, height = 6.5,
                             dpi = 300, bg = "white") {
  ggsave(
    filename = filename,
    plot = plot,
    width = width,
    height = height,
    units = "in",
    dpi = dpi,
    bg = bg
  )
}

# Standardize all plots for your thesis
theme_thesis <- function(base_size = 16) {
  theme_bw(base_size = base_size) +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = base_size + 2),
      axis.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = base_size),
      axis.text = element_text(color = "black", size = base_size - 1),
      legend.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = base_size - 1),
      legend.text = element_text(size = base_size - 2),
      strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = base_size - 1)
    )
}

```


* Input htseq count 
data acquired from htseq_count (run by htseq_count on VScode - cluster - address: )
** Prepare a table containing sample name, its conditions (which knock out), and its replicates
```{r}
# Input path
countFolder <- "/Users/tinkienoob/Master_thesis/MATR3/M3_htseqcount_nonreverse"

list.files(countFolder)
# List all file containing "counts" in the folder
countFiles <- list.files(countFolder, pattern = "htseq_forward")

# Create sample table/ data frame for DESeq from Htseq-count
## remove suffix in htseq output name
sampleNames <- sub("_htseq_forward.txt", "", countFiles)

# Split the string by the underscore "_"
meta_data_parts <- strsplit(sampleNames, "_")

# Extract the first part as condition (e.g., "dKO", "Matr3-cKO")
conditions <- sapply(meta_data_parts, function(sample_name) {
  paste(sample_name[1:(length(sample_name)-1)], collapse = "_")
})

# Extract the LAST part as the replicate
replicates <- sapply(meta_data_parts, function(sample_name) {
  sample_name[length(sample_name)]
})

# Create the df
sampleTable <- data.frame(
  sampleName = sampleNames,
  fileName   = countFiles,
  condition  = factor(conditions),
  replicate  = factor(replicates)
)

sampleTable$condition <- relevel(sampleTable$condition, ref = "WT")
```


* Create the DESeqDataSet (without matrix)
```{r}
dds <- DESeqDataSetFromHTSeqCount(sampleTable = sampleTable,
                                 directory = countFolder,
                                 design = ~ condition)
```

```{r}
# Check levels of condition
levels(dds$condition)

# Relevel to set WT as reference level
dds$condition <- relevel(dds$condition, ref = "WT")
```

* Running DESeq2 analysis to identify differentially expressed genes
```{r save-dds, eval=FALSE}
# Deseq data object 
dds = DESeq(dds)

# Check the names of results available
resultsNames(dds)

# Save dds outout in the outpath as rds file
saveRDS(dds, file = paste0(outpath, "dds_DESeq2_M3_RBM20.dds"))
```


# DESeq result
```{r} 
# Load the saved RDS file
dds <- readRDS(file = paste0(outpath, "dds_DESeq2_M3_RBM20.dds"))

# Identify all unique levels in your condition factor
groups <- levels(dds$condition)

# Generate all possible pairwise combinations
comparisons <- combn(groups, 2)

# Iterate through the combinations and run results()
all_res <- apply(comparisons, 2, function(pair) {
  results(dds, contrast = c("condition", pair[2], pair[1]), alpha = 0.05)
})

names(all_res) <- apply(comparisons, 2, function(pair) paste0(pair[2], "_vs_", pair[1]))

names(all_res) <- gsub("-", "_", names(all_res)) 
```

## Annotate all genes with R mouse genome package
There are probably many geneIds having the same gene name or it also could be same geneIds but different versions having the same gene name which create multiple similar geneIds in the gene annotation package. The version of the genes is used to track changes over time due to updates, transcript or feature changes, etc. 

The GRCm39 (mm39) mouse genome assembly contains approximately 50,763 total genes, of which 22,198 are protein-coding genes according to the NCBI RefSeq annotation. 


```{r}
process_results_list <- function(res_obj, res_name, dds, gene_anno_df, output_dir) {
  
  # Convert to dataframe and keep gene_id
  res_df <- as.data.frame(res_obj) %>%
    tibble::rownames_to_column("gene_id")

  # Split "GroupA_vs_GroupB" back into a vector
  compared_data <- strsplit(res_name, "_vs_")[[1]]
  contrast_vec <- c("condition", compared_data[2], compared_data[1])

  # Shrinkage
  res_ashr <- lfcShrink(dds = dds, contrast = contrast_vec, res = res_obj, type = "ashr")
  
  res_ashr_df <- as.data.frame(res_ashr) %>%
    tibble::rownames_to_column("gene_id") %>%
    dplyr::select(gene_id, log2FoldChange)
  
  # Merge shrinkage and annotations
  res_df <- res_df %>%
    dplyr::left_join(res_ashr_df, by = "gene_id", suffix = c("", ".ashr")) %>%
    dplyr::left_join(gene_anno_df, by = "gene_id")
  
  # Add Regulation threshold
  res_df <- res_df %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      regulated = !is.na(padj) & padj < 0.05,
      up_down = dplyr::case_when(
        log2FoldChange.ashr > 0 & regulated ~ "up",
        log2FoldChange.ashr < 0 & regulated ~ "down",
        TRUE ~ "not regulated"
      )
    ) %>%
    dplyr::arrange(desc(log2FoldChange.ashr))

  # Save individual RData for each comparison
  file_path <- file.path(output_dir, paste0("dds_res_", res_name, ".RData"))
  save(res_df, file = file_path)
  
  return(res_df)
}
```

setNames(): sets the names on an object and returns the object. In this case, it is used to assign names to the list of processed results based on the original comparison names. This allows you to easily access each processed results dataframe by its corresponding comparison name (e.g., dds_res_list$Matr3_cKO_vs_WT).
```{r skip-this-chunk, eval = FALSE}
# Process and save each results object
dds_res_list <- setNames(lapply(names(all_res), function(res_name) {
  process_results_list(
    res_obj = all_res[[res_name]],
    res_name = res_name,
    dds = dds,
    gene_anno_df = gene_anno_df,
    output_dir = "/Users/tinkienoob/Master_thesis/MATR3/Deseq2/"
  )
}), names(all_res))

#head(dds_res_list$dKO_vs_WT)

res_WT_vs_M3 <- dds_res_list$Matr3_cKO_vs_WT %>%
  mutate(GeneID = sub("(.+)\\..*", "\\1", gene_id))

res_WT_vs_dKO <- dds_res_list$dKO_vs_WT %>%
  mutate(GeneID = sub("(.+)\\..*", "\\1", gene_id))

res_WT_vs_RBM20 <- dds_res_list$RBM20_KO_vs_WT %>%
  mutate(GeneID = sub("(.+)\\..*", "\\1", gene_id))

# save all dds
save(res_WT_vs_M3, res_WT_vs_dKO, res_WT_vs_RBM20,
     file = "/Users/tinkienoob/Master_thesis/MATR3/Deseq2/dds_df_full/fulldds_DESeq2.RData")
```

* Multiple testing correction:
To reduce false positives when testing thousands of genes simultaneously. In this analysis, Benjamini-Hochberg (false discovery rate) is applied to control for multiple hypothesis testing.
Also DESeq2 usually applies median-of-ratios normalization (size Factors) internally when modeling counts for differential expression.
```{r include=FALSE}
### Information about which variables and tests were used can be found by calling the function mcols on the results object.
#mcols(res)$description
```

# EXPLONATORY DATA ANALYSIS
## P value and P-adj distribution
Most of p-values distribute between 0 and 1, and a peak of small p-values on the left side, which indicate a uniform background and are not significant. In other words, majority of genes usually has non-differentially expressend genes giving a rough indication of false discovery rate (FDR). That is why p-value is adjusted/ corrected for false discovery rate. False positives (with high p-value) are reduced after multiple testing.
Source: https://www.huber.embl.de/msmb/08-chap.html 
The highest bar  after adjustment is the most significant, others are insignificant. P-adj value is more common to use. In this analysis, we could tell most of counts having padj < 0.05, threshhold is set: padj < 0.05.
```{r include = FALSE}
# Create a function for histogram plot
# Store your DESeq2 results in a named list
mydds_res_list <- list(
  "Matr3_cKO_vs_WT" = res_WT_vs_M3,
  "dKO_vs_WT" = res_WT_vs_dKO,
  "RBM20_KO_vs_WT" = res_WT_vs_RBM20
)

myColors <- c(
      "WT" = "#009E73",
      "Matr3_cKO" = "dodgerblue3",
      "dKO" = "firebrick3", 
      "RBM20_KO" = "#FFCC65") 

# Your existing function (slightly optimized for the loop)
histogram_plot <- function(res_df, comp_name){ 
  res_df <- as.data.frame(res_df) %>% filter(!is.na(pvalue))
  
  hist1 <- ggplot(res_df, aes(x = pvalue)) +
    geom_histogram(fill = "dodgerblue3", color = "black", bins = 50) + 
    labs(title = paste(comp_name, "P-value"), x = "p-value", y = "Count") +
    theme_linedraw()
  
  hist2 <- ggplot(res_df, aes(x = padj)) + 
    geom_histogram(fill = "pink", color = "black", bins = 50) + 
    labs(title = paste(comp_name, "Adj P-value"), x = "padj", y = "Count") +
    theme_linedraw()
  
  return(gridExtra::arrangeGrob(hist1, hist2, ncol = 2))
}
```

```{r, results='asis', fig.width=10, fig.height=4, warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}
for (comp in names(mydds_res_list)) {
  # Create the tab heading
  cat("\n### ", comp, "{.tabset}\n")
  
  # Generate and print the plot
  p <- histogram_plot(mydds_res_list[[comp]], comp)
  grid::grid.newpage()
  grid::grid.draw(p)
  
  # Add space between tabs
  cat("\n\n")
}
```


## Hierarchical Clustering Heatmap/ Sample distance heatmap
This heatmap shows how different between all samples for evaluating strong patterns between conditions and potential outliers based on normalized gene expression values which is complementary to PCA. Clustered samples, hence, are expected to be similar to PCA plot observation.

* Log-transformed data to stabilize variance and
blind = F to not ignore differences in conditions and biological variation.
```{r}
# Perform rlog transformation
rld = rlog(dds, blind = F) 

# Compute pairwise distances between samples
sampleDist = dist(t(assay(rld)))
sampleMatrix = as.matrix(sampleDist)

# Make a color gradient palette for heatmap
color = colorRampPalette(rev(brewer.pal(9, "Blues")))(255)

# Create heatmap using pheatmap (simple)
annotation_df <- as.data.frame(colData(dds)[, "condition", drop = FALSE])

annotation_colors <- list(
  condition = myColors
)

pheatmap(
  sampleMatrix,
  col = color,
  clustering_distance_rows = sampleDist,
  clustering_distance_cols = sampleDist,
  annotation_col = annotation_df,
  annotation_row = annotation_df,
  annotation_colors = annotation_colors,
  main = "Sample Distance Heatmap (rlog transformed)"
)
```
dKO samples are clustering on a major branch with Matr3-cKO, while RBM20-KO sits on a more distant branch. It might indicate that the loss of Matr3 might be the dominant driver of the phenotype, or that RBM20's targets are a more specific subset of the Matr3 network.

# PCA
Principal Component Analysis (PCA) helps assess global gene expression patterns across samples. PCA plot is useful for visualizing relationships between samples based on their gene expression profiles which then can be evaluated whether the effect of experimental covariates or detection of batch effects.
What is a batch effect? The batch effect is usually caused by an unbalanced experimental design, which has the same procedure but different facilities/ reagents, equipment, etc. (non-biological reasons).
Samples with replicates that are similar in their gene expression will cluster close together; meanwhile, the more different the expression, the more separated the samples. PC1 (first principal component) is mostly aligned with the experimental covariate of interest or captures the most largest variance in data (between control and KD samples). Followed by PC2, the second component, is aligned with the sequencing protocol (between replicates) that captures technical variation helping identify batch effects.
```{r}
# PCA data
PCAdata <- plotPCA(
  rld,
  intgroup = c("condition", "replicate"),
  ntop = 5000,
  returnData = TRUE
)

percentVariance <- round(attr(PCAdata, "percentVar") * 100, 2)
PCAdata$sample_label <- paste0(PCAdata$condition, "_rep", PCAdata$replicate)

# Gender map
gender_map <- tibble::tribble(
  ~sample_label,        ~gender,
  "Matr3_cKO_rep1",     "Male",
  "Matr3_cKO_rep2",     "Male",
  "Matr3_cKO_rep3",     "Female",
  "dKO_rep1",           "Male",
  "dKO_rep2",           "Male",
  "dKO_rep3",           "Male",
  "RBM20_KO_rep1",      "Female",
  "RBM20_KO_rep2",      "Male",
  "RBM20_KO_rep3",      "Male",
  "WT_rep1",            "Male",
  "WT_rep2",            "Female",
  "WT_rep3",            "Male"
)

# Join gender into PCA data
PCAdata <- PCAdata %>%
  dplyr::left_join(gender_map, by = "sample_label")

gender_shapes <- c("Female" = 17, "Male" = 16)  # triangle = F, circle = M

# PCA plot — color = condition, shape = gender
p_pca <- ggplot(PCAdata, aes(PC1, PC2, color = condition, shape = gender)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey60") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey60") +
  geom_point(size = 4) +
  geom_text_repel(
    aes(label = sample_label),
    size = 4.5,
    fontface = "bold",
    max.overlaps = Inf,
    box.padding = 0.4,
    point.padding = 0.3,
    segment.color = "grey50",
    show.legend = FALSE
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = myColors) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = gender_shapes) +
  labs(
    title = "",
    x = paste0("PC1: ", percentVariance[1], "%"),
    y = paste0("PC2: ", percentVariance[2], "%"),
    color = "Condition",
    shape = "Gender"
  ) +
  theme_linedraw(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    plot.title   = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 16),
    axis.title   = element_text(face = "bold", size = 18),
    axis.text.x  = element_text(color = "black", size = 16),
    axis.text.y  = element_text(color = "black", size = 16),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"),
    legend.text  = element_text(size = 16),
    legend.position = "right"   # keep legend so gender is interpretable
  )

p_pca

save_thesis_plot(
  p_pca,
  "/Users/tinkienoob/Master_thesis/MATR3/Deseq2/PCA/PCA_plot_gender.png",
  width = 7, height = 6
)

```


# Count normalized reads of GOI for validation of RNA-seq results.
```{r}
dds_count_df <- counts(dds, normalized = TRUE) %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  rownames_to_column(var = "GeneID")


gender_shapes <- c("Female" = 17, "Male" = 16)  # triangle = female, circle = male

sample_meta_gender <- as.data.frame(colData(dds)) %>%
  rownames_to_column(var = "sample") %>%
  mutate(
    sample_label = paste0(condition, "_rep", replicate)
  ) %>%
  left_join(gender_map, by = "sample_label")

mygenes <- gene_anno_df %>%
  filter(gene_name %in% c("Matr3", "Rbm20")) %>%
  mutate(myLabel = gene_name)

KOgene_normalcounts <- dds_count_df %>%
  filter(GeneID %in% mygenes$gene_id) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols      = -GeneID,
    names_to  = "sample",
    values_to = "norm_count"
  ) %>%
  left_join(sample_meta_gender, by = "sample") %>%
  left_join(
    mygenes %>% dplyr::select(gene_id, gene_name),
    by = c("GeneID" = "gene_id")
  )

# Dotplot of normalized counts
p_ko_counts <- ggplot(
  KOgene_normalcounts,
  aes(x = condition, y = norm_count, color = condition, shape = gender)
) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.2, size = 3.5, alpha = 0.8) +
  facet_wrap(~ gene_name, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_color_manual(values = myColors) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = gender_shapes) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
  labs(
    title  = "",
    x      = "Condition",
    y      = "Normalized Counts",
    color  = "",
    shape  = "Gender"
  ) +
    theme(
        strip.text   = element_text(size = 20, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 18),
    axis.text.x = element_text(color = "black", size = 16, angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.y = element_text(color = "black", size = 16),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 16)
  )

p_ko_counts

save_thesis_plot(p_ko_counts, "/Users/tinkienoob/Master_thesis/MATR3/Deseq2/PCA/normalized_count_genes.png", 
                 width = 10, height = 6)
```

How are top targets expressed? L2FC 
Global distribution of L2FC across different conditions
```{r}
global_violin_df <- bind_rows(
  res_WT_vs_M3 %>% mutate(cond = "Matr3_cKO_vs_WT"),
  res_WT_vs_dKO %>% mutate(cond = "dKO_vs_WT"),
  res_WT_vs_RBM20 %>% mutate(cond = "RBM20_KO_vs_WT")
)

mydata_colors <- c(
      "WT" = "#009E73",
      "Matr3_cKO_vs_WT" = "dodgerblue3",
      "dKO_vs_WT" = "firebrick3", 
      "RBM20_KO_vs_WT" = "#FFCC65"
    )

ggplot(global_violin_df, aes(x = cond, y = log2FoldChange, fill = cond)) +
  geom_violin(trim = FALSE, alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.12, fill = "white", outlier.shape = NA, alpha = 0.3) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = mydata_colors) +
  labs(
    title = "Global Expression changes across different conditions",
    y = "Log2 Fold Change (Knockout vs WT)",
    x = ""
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5),
    legend.position = "none"
  ) + 
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(-3, 3))
```
double knockout disrupts the transcriptome more severely than either single knockout, leading to more extreme expression changes in specific targets. Meanwhile RBM20 KO seems to have least changes since L2FC distribution expand around 0.


# Upset plot
```{r}
# Custom function to generate high-quality UpSet plots with color-coded reproducibility
plot_reproducible_upset2 <- function(upset_list, filename, main_label, highlight_color = "#2171B5") {
  
  # ── 0. Make sure output directory exists ──────────────────────────────
  out_dir <- dirname(filename)
  if (!dir.exists(out_dir)) {
    dir.create(out_dir, recursive = TRUE)
    message("Created directory: ", out_dir)
  }
  
  # 1. Transform list to UpSet format
  upset_data <- UpSetR::fromList(upset_list)
  set_names  <- names(upset_list)
  
  # 2. Generate all combinations for intersections (degree >= 2)
  shared_combos <- unlist(lapply(2:length(set_names), function(x) {
    combn(set_names, x, simplify = FALSE)
  }), recursive = FALSE)
  
  # 3. Create queries for UpSetR
  highlight_queries <- lapply(shared_combos, function(combo) {
    list(query  = UpSetR::intersects,
         params = combo,
         color  = highlight_color,
         active = TRUE)
  })
  
  # 4. Render and Save Plot
  png(filename = filename, width = 2600, height = 1800, res = 300, bg = "white")
  
  p <- UpSetR::upset(
    upset_data,
    nsets           = length(set_names),
    order.by        = "freq",
    mainbar.y.label = main_label,
    sets.x.label    = "Editing sites / Replicate",
    sets.bar.color  = "#08306B",
    main.bar.color  = "#08306B",
    queries         = highlight_queries,
    text.scale      = c(2.0, 1.8, 1.8, 1.6, 2.0, 2.4),
  )
  
  print(p)
  dev.off()
}

```


```{r}
library(UpSetR)
# Prepare data for UpSet plot
upset_list <- list(
  "dKO"       = res_WT_vs_dKO[res_WT_vs_dKO$regulated == TRUE,]$GeneID,
  "Matr3" = res_WT_vs_M3[res_WT_vs_M3$regulated == TRUE,]$GeneID,
  "RBM20"  = res_WT_vs_RBM20[res_WT_vs_RBM20$regulated == TRUE,]$GeneID
)

upset_plot_rnaseq <- plot_reproducible_upset2(
  upset_list = upset_list,
  filename = "/Users/tinkienoob/Master_thesis/MATR3/Deseq2/UpSet/upset_rnaseq.png",
  main_label = "Significantly regulated genes"
)
```
Matr3 is the primary driver of the transcriptome in this context.


# Violin plot of L2FC distribution of high-confidence targets vs other genes
```{r}
principal_highconf_tx <- readRDS("/Users/tinkienoob/Master_thesis/MATR3/Hypertribe_bindingsites_analysis/principal_highconf_tx_df.rds")

# Violin plot of L2FC distribution of all genes 
highconf_violin_df <- global_violin_df %>%
  filter(cond == "Matr3_cKO_vs_WT") %>%
  filter(!is.na(log2FoldChange)) %>%
  mutate(category = ifelse(gene_id %in% rank_M3tx_df$gene_id, 
                           "High confidence targets", 
                           "others"))

ggplot(highconf_violin_df, aes(x = category, y = log2FoldChange, fill = category)) +
  geom_violin(trim = FALSE, alpha = 0.4) +
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.12, fill = "white", alpha = 0.4, outlier.shape = NA) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      "High confidence targets" = "dodgerblue3",
      "others" = "grey80")) +
  labs(
    title = "Expression changes of high-confidence targets vs other genes",
    subtitle = paste(
      "High-confidence targets:", sum(highconf_violin_df$category == "High confidence targets"),
      "| Other genes:", sum(highconf_violin_df$category == "others")),
    y = "Log2 Fold Change (Matr3-cKO vs WT)",
    x = "") +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5),
    legend.position = "none") + 
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(-2, 2))

```


Do my top targets behave differently than the rest of the genome upon Matrin3 knockdown?
```{r}
ranking_plot_df <- highconf_violin_df %>%
  left_join(
    rank_M3tx_df %>% dplyr::select(gene_id, rank),
    by = "gene_id") %>%
  filter(!is.na(log2FoldChange)) %>%
  mutate(
    rank = ifelse(is.na(rank), "Others", rank),
    rank = ifelse(is.na(rank), "Others", as.character(rank)),
    rank = factor(rank, levels = c("High", "Intermediate", "Low", "Others")))

# Global Colors
tier_colors <- c(
  "High" = "#CD534C", 
  "Intermediate" = "#EFC050", 
  "Low" = "#45B7D1",
  "Others" = "grey80"
)

# Global Comparisons
tier_comparisons <- list(
  c("High", "Intermediate"),
  c("High", "Low"),
  c("Intermediate", "Low")
)

c1 <- ggplot(ranking_plot_df, aes(x = rank, y = log2FoldChange, fill = rank)) +
  #geom_violin(trim = FALSE, alpha = 0.7, color = NA) +
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.7, alpha = 0.7, outlier.shape = NA) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "black") +
  stat_compare_means(
    comparisons = tier_comparisons,
    method = "wilcox.test",
    aes(label = paste0("p=", after_stat(p.format))),
    label.y = c(0.4, 0.575, 0.75),
  size = 6.5) + 
  scale_fill_manual(values = tier_colors) + 
  theme_bw(base_size = 16) +
  theme_thesis() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"),
        axis.title.y = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"),
        plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5),
        legend.position = "none") +
  labs(y = "Log2 Fold Change (MATR3-cKO vs WT)",
       x = "") +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(-0.5, 1.5))

c1

save_thesis_plot(c1, "/Users/tinkienoob/Master_thesis/MATR3/Deseq2/violintier_L2FC_targets.png", width = 6, height = 7)
```
p-value between each pair of data. 
ggpubr() for stat_compare_mean(), eg. t-test to compare significant whether L2fc significantly dif froom Tier 2 and tier 3

majority of genes are not changing between your KO and WT samples. Matrin3 binding does NOT predict a change in gene expression.

Multiple studies show Matrin3 is a nuclear matrix splicing regulator: it binds intronic pyrimidine‑rich regions and represses or modulates cassette exon inclusion across hundreds of alternative splicing events, often without major changes in total gene expression. 
=> enriched for Skipped Exons (SE) or Retained Introns (RI).
=> Differential Splicing Results

Matrin3 also participates in RNA processing more broadly (RNA stability for a limited subset of mRNAs, nuclear retention of hyper‑edited RNAs, interactions with PABPN1 and other RNA‑processing factors), again emphasizing processing and quality control rather than global up‑ or downregulation of mRNA levels.
```{r}
####################### Create x and y-axis limits for the zoomed-in plot
zoom_xlim <- c(-0.5, 0)
zoom_ylim <- c(0, 0.4)

####################### Create the main ECDF plot
c2 <- ggplot(
  ranking_plot_df,
  aes(x = log2FoldChange, color = rank)
) +
  stat_ecdf(
    geom = "point",
    aes(y = after_stat(y)),
    alpha = 0.6,
    size = 0.8,
    position = "identity"
  ) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "black") +
  scale_color_manual(values = tier_colors) +
  labs(
    title = "",
    x = "Log2 Fold Change (MATR3-cKO vs WT)",
    y = "Cumulative probability",
    color = "Confidence level"
  ) +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(-2.5, 2.5), ylim = c(0, 1)) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
    legend.background = element_rect(fill = alpha("white", 0.35), color = NA),
    legend.key = element_blank()
  )
```

# ECDF
A curve that is more to the left means that group contains a larger fraction of more negative log2 fold changes, so those genes tend to be more downregulated in Matr3-cKO vs WT. A curve that is more to the right means values are less negative or closer to 0. However, others (grey color) shows more significant downregulation meanwhile it is actually not significant. //
The cumulative distribution of L2FC for high-confidence targets (Tier 1, Tier 2, Tier 3) is shifted to the left compared to other genes, indicating that these targets tend to be more downregulated in the Matr3-cKO condition compared to WT. This suggests that Matrin3 may play a role in maintaining the expression of its high-confidence targets, and their downregulation upon Matrin3 knockout could be a direct consequence of losing Matrin3's regulatory function on these genes. However, the majority of genes (grey curve) do not show this pattern, reinforcing the idea that Matrin3's effect is specific to its high-confidence targets rather than causing widespread changes in gene expression.

```{r}
####################### Add a rectangle to indicate the zoomed-in area on the main plot
c2_main <- c2 +
  annotate(
    "rect",
    xmin = zoom_xlim[1], xmax = zoom_xlim[2],
    ymin = zoom_ylim[1], ymax = zoom_ylim[2],
    alpha = 0.08,
    fill = "white",
    color = "red",
    linetype = 2, size = 0.7
  ) +
  theme(
  axis.title.x  = element_text(size = 15, face = "bold"),
  axis.title.y  = element_text(size = 15, face = "bold"),
  axis.text.x   = element_text(size = 16),
  axis.text.y   = element_text(size = 16),
  plot.title    = element_text(size = 20, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
  legend.title  = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"),
  legend.text   = element_text(size = 14),
  legend.key.size = unit(1.0, "cm"),
  legend.position = "none"
)

####################### Create the zoomed-in plot (c1) and inset it into the main plot (c2)
c2_main_inset <- c2_main +
  inset_element(
    c1,
    left = 0.05,
    bottom = 0.4,
    right = 0.98,
    top = 0.05
  )

####################### extract legend separately
legend_plot <- c2 +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.direction = "vertical",
    legend.title = element_text(size = 11, face = "bold"),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 11),
    legend.background = element_rect(fill = alpha("white", 0.8), color = NA)
  )

tier_legend <- get_legend(legend_plot)

####################### Create the zoomed-in plot (c2_zoom) with the same data but zoomed in on the specified x and y limits
c2_zoom <- c2 +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = zoom_xlim, ylim = zoom_ylim) +
  labs(title = "zoom-in area", x = NULL, y = NULL) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    plot.title = element_text(size = 14, hjust = 0, face = "bold")
  )

####################### Combine the main plot with the inset and the zoomed-in plot side by side
main_with_inset <- ggdraw() +
  draw_plot(c2_main, x = 0, y = 0, width = 1, height = 1) +
  #draw_plot(c1, x = 0.17, y = 0.57, width = 0.34, height = 0.32) +   # top-left inset
  draw_plot(tier_legend, x = 0.7, y = 0.4, width = 0.24, height = 0.20)  # bottom-right legend

####################### combine left and right panels
ecdf_regulate <- ggdraw() +
  # left = main plot with inset
  draw_plot(main_with_inset, x = 0, y = 0, width = 0.66, height = 1) +
  # right = zoom plot
  draw_plot(c2_zoom, x = 0.645, y = 0.12, width = 0.37, height = 0.8) +
  draw_line(
    x = c(0.38, 0.67),
    y = 0.3,
    linetype = "dashed",
    color = "red",
    size = 0.7)

ecdf_regulate

save_thesis_plot(ecdf_regulate, "/Users/tinkienoob/Master_thesis/MATR3/Deseq2/ECDF_plot.png", 
                  width = 9, height = 6)
```




Tier1 (red) is the most left-shifted among the three tiers in the zoomed region.
This suggests Tier1 has the strongest negative shift overall.
In other words, Tier1 targets appear to be most downregulated in the knockout.
Tier2 (gold) is intermediate.
It is still clearly left of 0 and follows the same general pattern as Tier1/Tier3.
Its downregulation looks weaker than Tier1 but stronger than or similar to Tier3, depending on the x-range you focus on.
Tier3 (blue) is slightly more right-shifted than Tier1, and often a bit right of Tier2 too.
That suggests Tier3 is the least negatively shifted of the three tiers.
So Tier3 targets still trend down, but less strongly.
Others (gray) behave very differently.
In the full ECDF, the gray curve is much broader and spread across a wider range.
That means the “Others” group is more heterogeneous: it contains many genes with stronger negative values, but also many values extending toward 0 and positive L2FC.
So unlike Tier1–3, which look tightly concentrated in a narrow negative range, “Others” seem less coherent as a class.



# Venn diagram of significant genes vs high-confidence targets
```{r}
highconf_tx_overlap_signGenes <- list(
  "Matr3-cKO"  = unique(res_WT_vs_M3$GeneID[res_WT_vs_M3$regulated == TRUE]),
  "RBM20-KO"  = unique(res_WT_vs_RBM20$GeneID[res_WT_vs_RBM20$regulated == TRUE]),
  "dKO" = unique(res_WT_vs_dKO$GeneID[res_WT_vs_dKO$regulated == TRUE]),
  "High conf tx" = unique(ranking_plot_df$GeneID[ranking_plot_df$category == "High confidence targets"])
)

ggvenn(
  highconf_tx_overlap_signGenes, 
  fill_color = c("#BD241E", "cornflowerblue", "#F5B355", "#009E73"), # Your specific colors
  fill_alpha = 0.5,    # Transparency (0 to 1)
  stroke_size = 0.5,   # Thickness of the circle outlines
  set_name_size = 4,   # Size of the set labels (Matr3-cKO, etc.)
  text_size = 3,       # Size of the numbers inside the intersections
  show_percentage = FALSE # Set to TRUE if you want % alongside counts
) +
  ggplot2::labs(title = "Overlap of Regulated Genes & High Confidence Targets") +
  ggplot2::theme(plot.title = ggplot2::element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"))
```


```{r}
# Function to create an UpSet plot for a given dataset and its high-confidence targets
reghighconf_upset_plot <- function(res_df, dataset_name, high_conf_df) {
  
  overlap_list <- list(
    unique(res_df$gene_id[res_df$up_down == "up"]),
    unique(res_df$gene_id[res_df$up_down == "down"]),
    unique(high_conf_df$gene_id[high_conf_df$rank == "Tier1"]), 
    unique(high_conf_df$gene_id[high_conf_df$rank == "Tier2"]),
    unique(high_conf_df$gene_id[high_conf_df$rank == "Tier3"])
    )
  
  # Assign names AFTER creating list
  names(overlap_list) <- c(
    paste0(dataset_name, "_Up"),
    paste0(dataset_name, "_Down"),
    "Tier1", "Tier2", "Tier3")
  
  p <- UpSetR::upset(
    UpSetR::fromList(overlap_list),
    nsets = length(overlap_list),
    order.by = "freq",
    mainbar.y.label = paste("Shared genes in", dataset_name),
    sets.x.label = "Significantly regulated genes",
    sets.bar.color = "#64B5F6",
    main.bar.color = "#0E54B6",
    text.scale = c(1.2, 1.2, 1, 1, 1, 1)
  )
  
  print(p)
}
```


```{r results='asis'}
cat("## Differential Expression Genes of each tier {.tabset}\n\n")

for (dataset_name in names(mydds_res_list)) {
  
  cat("###", dataset_name, "\n\n")
  
  reghighconf_upset_plot(
    res_df = mydds_res_list[[dataset_name]],
    dataset_name = dataset_name,
    high_conf_df = ranking_plot_df
  )
  
  cat("\n\n")
}
```

```{r go-analysis-function}
# plot_go_enrichment <- function(ego_obj, set_name, n_terms = 6, n_show_genes = 3) {
#   
#   plot_df <- as.data.frame(ego_obj) %>%
#     tidyr::separate(GeneRatio, into = c("gene_count", "bg_count"), sep = "/", convert = TRUE) %>%

#       geneID = as.character(geneID)

#       is.finite(GeneRatio_num),

#       !is.na(Description),
#       !is.na(ONTOLOGY)

#       # }),

#       ONTOLOGY = dplyr::recode(ONTOLOGY,
#         "BP" = "Biological Process",

#         "MF" = "Molecular Function"

#   #                           levels = term_label[order(ONTOLOGY, GeneRatio_num)]))

#

#   plot_df$ONTOLOGY <- factor(plot_df$ONTOLOGY, 
#                               levels = c("Biological Process", 
#                                          "Cellular Component", 
#                                          "Molecular Function"))

#   ggplot(
#     plot_df,
#     aes(
#       x = GeneRatio_num,
#       y = reorder(term_label, GeneRatio_num),
#       color = p.adjust,
#       size = Count)) +
#     geom_point(alpha = 0.6) +
#     ggrepel::geom_text_repel(
#       aes(label = gene_label),
#       size = 4,
#       color = "black",
#       segment.color = "grey70",
#       min.segment.length = 0,
#       direction = "y",
#       hjust = 0,
#       nudge_x = 0.004,
#       box.padding = 0.2,
#       max.overlaps = Inf,
#       show.legend = FALSE) +
#     facet_grid(ONTOLOGY ~ ., scales = "free_y", space = "free_y") +
#     scale_color_continuous(low = "red", high = "blue", trans = "reverse") +
#     
#     # ---- CHANGE 2: Rename size and x-axis labels ----
#     scale_size_continuous(
#       name = "Number of\nMATR3 targets"   # renames the Count legend
#     ) +
#     
#     expand_limits(x = max(plot_df$GeneRatio_num, na.rm = TRUE) + 0.015) +
#     labs(
#       title = paste("Functional Enrichment of Matrin3", set_name, "genes"),
#       
#       # ---- CHANGE 2 continued: Rename x-axis ----
#       x = "Ratio of MATR3 targets to total genes in respective terms",
#       y = NULL,
#       
#       # ---- CHANGE 3: Rename color legend title ----
#       color = "Adjusted\np-value"
#     ) +
#     theme_light(base_size = 15) +
#     theme(
#       plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
#       strip.background = element_rect(fill = "grey20"),
#       strip.text = element_text(color = "white", face = "bold"),
#       axis.text.y = element_text(size = 15),
#       plot.margin = margin(10, 90, 10, 10))
# }
```

# without gene names
```{r}
plot_go_enrichment <- function(ego_obj, set_name, n_terms = 6) {  # remove n_show_genes argument
  
  plot_df <- as.data.frame(ego_obj) %>%
    tidyr::separate(GeneRatio, into = c("gene_count", "bg_count"), sep = "/", convert = TRUE) %>%
    mutate(
      GeneRatio_num = gene_count / bg_count,
      ONTOLOGY = as.character(ONTOLOGY),
      geneID = as.character(geneID)
    ) %>%
    filter(
      !is.na(GeneRatio_num),
      is.finite(GeneRatio_num),
      !is.na(p.adjust),
      !is.na(Count),
      !is.na(Description),
      !is.na(ONTOLOGY)
    ) %>%
    group_by(ONTOLOGY) %>%
    slice_min(p.adjust, n = n_terms, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
    ungroup() %>%
    mutate(
      term_label = str_wrap(Description, width = 32),
      
      # ---- REMOVED: gene_label generation ----
      
      ONTOLOGY = dplyr::recode(ONTOLOGY,
        "BP" = "Biological Process",
        "CC" = "Cellular Component",
        "MF" = "Molecular Function"
      )
    )
  
  plot_df$ONTOLOGY <- factor(plot_df$ONTOLOGY, 
                              levels = c("Biological Process", 
                                         "Cellular Component", 
                                         "Molecular Function"))
  
  ggplot(
    plot_df,
    aes(
      x = GeneRatio_num,
      y = reorder(term_label, GeneRatio_num),
      color = p.adjust,
      size = Count)) +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.6) +

    
    facet_grid(ONTOLOGY ~ ., scales = "free_y", space = "free_y") +
    scale_color_continuous(low = "red", high = "blue", trans = "reverse") +
    scale_size_continuous(name = "Number of\nMATR3 Targets") +
  
    
    labs(
      title = paste("Functional Enrichment of Matrin3", set_name, "genes"),
      x = "Ratio of MATR3 Targets to Total Genes in Term",
      y = NULL,
      color = "Adjusted\np-value"
    ) +
    theme_light(base_size = 15) +
    theme(
        strip.text   = element_text(size = 18, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 18),
    axis.text.x = element_text(color = "black", size = 16),
    axis.text.y = element_text(color = "black", size = 16),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 16),
      plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
      strip.background = element_rect(fill = "grey20"),
      plot.margin = margin(10, 10, 10, 10))  # reduced right margin since no labels
}

```

# GO term analyis
```{r}
# Define your tiers and a static universe 
tiers <- c("High" = "High", "Intermediate" = "Intermediate", "Low" = "Low")
M3_universe <- res_WT_vs_M3 %>% filter(baseMean > 5) %>% pull(GeneID)

remove_go_ids <- c("GO:0042692")   # example: muscle cell differentiation

for (tier_label in names(tiers)) {
  tier <- tiers[[tier_label]]
  
  # Create the tabset header
  cat("\n## ", tier_label, "\n")
  
  # Run the enrichment
  ego <- enrichGO(
    gene          = rank_M3tx_df[rank_M3tx_df$rank == tier,]$GeneID,
    universe      = M3_universe,
    OrgDb         = org.Mm.eg.db,
    keyType       = "ENSEMBL",  
    ont           = "ALL",      
    pAdjustMethod = "BH",       
    pvalueCutoff  = 0.1,
    qvalueCutoff  = 0.2,
    readable      = TRUE
  )

  p <- plot_go_enrichment(ego, tier_label, n_terms = 6)
  
   print(p)
  
  cat("\n\n")
}
```



# MA Plot
```{r ma-plot}
cat("## MA Plots {.tabset}\n\n")

for (name in names(mydds_res_list)) {

  cat("###", gsub("_", " ", name), "\n\n")

  # Access the specific results dataframe
  res_df <- mydds_res_list[[name]] %>%
    filter(baseMean > 5) %>%
    mutate(
      is_tier1 = gene_id %in%
        rank_M3tx_df[rank_M3tx_df$rank == "Tier1", ]$gene_id
    )

  plot <- ggplot(res_df, aes(x = baseMean, y = log2FoldChange.ashr)) +

    geom_point(
      aes(color = up_down),
      size = 1.5,
      alpha = 0.4
    ) +

   scale_x_log10(
  breaks = scales::breaks_log(),
  labels = scales::label_math(10^.x, format = log10),
  expand = expansion(mult = c(0.02, 0.05))
) +

    geom_hline(
      yintercept = 0,
      color = "black",
      linetype = "dashed"
    ) +

    scale_color_manual(values = c(
      "up" = "firebrick3",
      "down" = "dodgerblue3",
      "not regulated" = "grey80"
    )) +

   # coord_cartesian(ylim = c(-1.5, 2)) +
    labs(
      title = gsub("_", " ", name),
      x = "Mean of normalized counts",
      y = "Shrunken Log2 Fold Change"
    ) +

    theme_bw(base_size = 16) +
    theme_thesis() +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
      strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16),
      legend.position = "none"
    ) +

    stat_group_counts(
      data = ~ subset(.x, up_down == "up"),
      aes(group = up_down,
          label = paste("Up:", after_stat(count.label))),
      geom = "text_npc",
      npcx = 0.02,
      npcy = 0.95,
      hjust = -5,
      vjust = 1,
      size = 4
    ) +

    stat_group_counts(
      data = ~ subset(.x, up_down == "down"),
      aes(group = up_down,
          label = paste("Down:", after_stat(count.label))),
      geom = "text_npc",
      npcx = 0.02,
      npcy = 0.05,
      hjust = -4,
      vjust = 0,
      size = 4
    )

  print(plot)

  # create unique filename
  file_name <- paste0(
    "/Users/tinkienoob/Master_thesis/MATR3/Deseq2/",
    "MAplot_",
    name,
    ".png"
  )

  save_thesis_plot(
    plot,
    file_name,
    width = 6.5,
    height = 6
  )

  cat("\n\n")
}

```
# MA plot for Matrin3 only, faceted by tier
```{r}
# pick the Matrin3 result table from your list
MAplot_tier_M3vsWT <- res_WT_vs_M3 %>%
  filter(gene_id %in% rank_M3tx_df$gene_id) %>%
  left_join(
    rank_M3tx_df %>% dplyr::select(gene_id, rank),
    by = "gene_id") %>%
  mutate(
    rank = case_when(
      rank %in% c("High", "Intermediate", "Low") ~ as.character(rank),
      TRUE ~ "Others"),
    rank = factor(rank, levels = c("High", "Intermediate", "Low", "Others")))

tier_counts <- MAplot_tier_M3vsWT %>%
  group_by(rank) %>%
  summarise(
    total = n(),
    up    = sum(up_down == "up", na.rm = TRUE),
    down  = sum(up_down == "down", na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    up_label   = paste0("Up: ", up, "/", total),
    down_label = paste0("Down: ", down, "/", total)
  )

# 👉 compute per-facet positions
pos_df <- MAplot_tier_M3vsWT %>%
  group_by(rank) %>%
  summarise(
    x_pos = min(baseMean, na.rm = TRUE) * 1.2,
    y_top = max(log2FoldChange.ashr, na.rm = TRUE) * 0.95,
    y_bot = min(log2FoldChange.ashr, na.rm = TRUE) * 0.95,
    .groups = "drop"
  )

tier_counts <- left_join(tier_counts, pos_df, by = "rank")
```


```{r}
maplot_tier <- ggplot(
  MAplot_tier_M3vsWT,
  aes(x = baseMean, y = log2FoldChange.ashr)
) +
  
  # ── points ───────────────────────────────────
  geom_point(
    aes(color = up_down),
    size = 1.2,
    alpha = 0.5
  ) +
  
  # ── horizontal reference line ────────────────
  geom_hline(
    yintercept = 0,
    linetype = "dashed",
    color = "black"
  ) +
  
  # ── gene labels ──────────────────────────────
  # geom_label_repel(
  #   data = subset(MAplot_tier_M3vsWT, regulated & !is.na(gene_name)),
  #   aes(label = gene_name, color = up_down),
  #   size = 5,
  #   max.overlaps = 10,
  #   box.padding = 0.1,
  #   point.padding = 0.1,
  #   show.legend = FALSE
  # ) +
  
  # ── facet ────────────────────────────────────
  facet_wrap(~ rank, ncol = 3) +
  
  # ── UP label (top-right) ──────────────────────
  geom_text_npc(
    data = tier_counts,
    aes(label = up_label),
    npcx = 0.98,   # 👉 RIGHT side
    npcy = 0.98,   # 👉 TOP
    hjust = 1,     # align text to right edge
    vjust = 1,
    size = 6,
    inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +

  # ── DOWN label (bottom-right) ────────────────
  geom_text_npc(
    data = tier_counts,
    aes(label = down_label),
    npcx = 0.98,   # 👉 RIGHT side
    npcy = 0.05,   # 👉 BOTTOM
    hjust = 1,
    vjust = 0,
    size = 6,
    inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +
  
  scale_color_manual(values = c(
    "up" = "firebrick3",
    "down" = "dodgerblue3",
    "not regulated" = "grey80")) +
    
  # 🔥 UPDATED SCALE HERE 🔥
scale_x_log10(
    breaks = scales::breaks_log(), # Ensures major grid lines fall on powers of 10
    labels = scales::label_math(10^.x, format = log10), # Renders proper mathematical superscripts
    expand = expansion(mult = 0)
  ) +
  
  # ── limits ───────────────────────────────────
  coord_cartesian(
    ylim = c(-1, 1.5),
    xlim = c(6, NA)) +
  
  # ── labels ───────────────────────────────────
  labs(
    x = "Mean of normalized counts",
    y = "Shrunkage Log2 Fold Change (MATR3-cKO vs WT)",
    color = NULL) +
  
  # ── theme ────────────────────────────────────
  theme_bw(base_size = 16) +
  theme_thesis() +
  theme(
    plot.title   = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
    strip.text   = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16),
    legend.position = "none")

maplot_tier

save_thesis_plot(maplot_tier, "/Users/tinkienoob/Master_thesis/MATR3/Deseq2/MAplot_tier_M3vsWT.png", width = 14, height = 6)

```


```{r}
# define regulation classes from DE result
ranking_bar_df <- ranking_plot_df %>%
  mutate(
    regulation = case_when(
      padj < 0.05 & log2FoldChange > 0  ~ "Up",
      padj < 0.05 & log2FoldChange < 0  ~ "Down",
      TRUE                              ~ "Not regulated"
    ),
    rank = as.character(rank),
    rank = ifelse(is.na(rank), "Others", rank),
    rank = factor(rank, levels = c("High", "Intermediate", "Low", "Others")),
    regulation = factor(regulation, levels = c("Down", "Not regulated", "Up"))
  )

# count per group
rank_reg_summary <- ranking_bar_df %>%
  dplyr::count(rank, regulation, name = "n") %>%
  group_by(rank) %>%
  mutate(
    total = sum(n),
    prop = n / total
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

rank_reg_summary
```

# Volcano plot
```{r volcano-plot}
make_volcano_plot <- function(res_df, dataset_name, ranking_plot_df) {
  
  lim <- ceiling(max(abs(res_df$log2FoldChange), na.rm = TRUE) / 0.5) * 0.5
  
  volcano_df <- res_df %>%
    filter(!is.na(padj)) %>%
    mutate(
      tier1_transcripts = ifelse(
        GeneID %in% ranking_plot_df[
          ranking_plot_df$rank == "Tier1", ]$GeneID,
        gene_name, NA
      ),
      is_key_prot = gene_name %in% c("Matr3", "Rbm20"),
      neg_log10_padj = -log10(padj),
      padj_corrected = pmin(neg_log10_padj, 50)
    )
  
  ggplot(volcano_df,
         aes(x = log2FoldChange,
             y = padj_corrected,
             color = up_down)) +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.6, size = 1.5) +
    geom_label_repel(data = subset(volcano_df, regulated == TRUE & !is_key_prot), 
                     aes(label = gene_name), 
                     point.padding = 0.1, 
                     max.overlaps = 30,
                     size = 2.5, 
                     show.legend = FALSE) +
    geom_point(
      data = subset(volcano_df, !is.na(tier1_transcripts)), # Label tier 1 transcripts
      fill = "#FFCC65",
      color = "black",
      size = 2,
      shape = 21) +
    geom_label_repel(data = subset(volcano_df, !is.na(tier1_transcripts)), 
                     aes(label = gene_name), 
                     color = "black", 
                     fill = "#FFCC65", 
                     size = 3, 
                     box.padding = 0.5, 
                     max.overlaps = 20, 
                     show.legend = FALSE) +
    # LABELING for MATR3/RBM20 
    geom_label_repel(data = subset(volcano_df, is_key_prot), 
                     aes(label = gene_name),
                     fontface = "bold", 
                     size = 4, 
                     point.padding = 0.75, 
                     min.segment.length = 0) +
    scale_color_manual(values = c(
      "up" = "firebrick3",
      "down" = "dodgerblue3",
      "not regulated" = "grey80")) +
    scale_x_continuous(limits = c(-lim, lim)) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = -log10(0.05),
               linetype = "dashed") +
    geom_vline(xintercept = 0,
               linetype = "dashed") +
    theme_bw() +
    labs(
      title = paste("Volcano Plot:", gsub("_", " ", dataset_name)),
      subtitle = "Yellow = Tier 1 Targets",
      x = "Log2 Fold Change",
      y = "-Log10(padj)") + 
    # Quadrant counts for significant regulated genes and not regulated genes 
    stat_quadrant_counts(xintercept = 0, yintercept = -log10(0.05), 
                        aes(label = case_when(after_stat(quadrant) == 1 ~ paste0("Up: ", after_stat(count)), 
                                              after_stat(quadrant) == 2 ~ paste0("Not reg: ", after_stat(count)), 
                                              after_stat(quadrant) == 3 ~ paste0("Not reg: ", after_stat(count)), 
                                              after_stat(quadrant) == 4 ~ paste0("Down: ", after_stat(count)), 
                                              TRUE ~ NA_character_)), 
                          geom = "text", color = "black", size = 4 ) + 
    theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5))
}
```


```{r results='asis'}
cat("## Volcano Plots {.tabset}\n\n")

for (cond in names(mydds_res_list)) {
  
  cat("###", gsub("_", " ", cond), "\n\n")
  
  plot <- make_volcano_plot(
    res_df = mydds_res_list[[cond]],
    dataset_name = cond,
    ranking_plot_df = ranking_plot_df
  )
  
  print(plot)
  
  cat("\n\n")
}
```
Rbm20 is another splicing factor linked to cardiomyopathy. The upregulation of Rbm20 (a splicing regulator) strongly suggests a splicing network disruption.
MYH7 is a gene that provides instructions for making the beta-myosin heavy chain protein, a crucial component of cardiac (heart) and slow-twitch skeletal muscles, forming part of the molecular motor for contraction. 
ACTA2 refers to the Alpha-2 Smooth Muscle Actin gene, which provides instructions for making the actin alpha-2 protein, a crucial component in smooth muscle contraction, cell structure, and blood vessel health. Mutations in the ACTA2 gene are linked to serious genetic conditions like Multisystemic Smooth Muscle Dysfunction Syndrome (MSMDS), Thoracic Aortic Aneurysm and Dissection (TAAD), and premature heart/stroke issues, impacting blood pressure regulation and vascular integrity.

Although Matrin3’s HyperTRIBE‑defined top targets are bound and edited most strongly, their steady‑state expression is largely buffered between WT and cKO, suggesting 

# DEG lists
```{r, message=FALSE, warnings=FALSE}
Matr3cKO_sigGenes <- res_WT_vs_M3 %>% subset(regulated == TRUE) %>% pull(GeneID)
RBM20KO_sigGenes <- res_WT_vs_RBM20  %>% subset(regulated == TRUE) %>% pull(GeneID)
dKO_sigGenes <- res_WT_vs_dKO  %>% subset(regulated == TRUE) %>% pull(GeneID)
sigGenes_set <- c(Matr3cKO_sigGenes, RBM20KO_sigGenes, dKO_sigGenes) %>% unique(.)
```

# GO terms analysis
::: panel-tabset
## Significant upregulated genes
```{r go-analysis-up}
ego_M3_up <- enrichGO(
  gene = res_WT_vs_M3[res_WT_vs_M3$up_down == "up",]$GeneID,
  universe = res_WT_vs_M3$GeneID,
  OrgDb = org.Mm.eg.db,
  keyType = "ENSEMBL",
  ont = "ALL",
  pAdjustMethod = "BH",
  pvalueCutoff = 0.05,
  qvalueCutoff = 0.05,
  readable = TRUE
)
```


```{r}
ego_up_plot <- plot_go_enrichment(ego_M3_up, "upregulated", n_terms = 10)

save_thesis_plot(
  plot = ego_up_plot,
  filename = "/Users/tinkienoob/Master_thesis/MATR3/Deseq2/GO_deseq2_png/go_upregulated.png",
  width = 9,
  height = 14.5
)

ego_up_plot
```

## Significant downregulated genes
```{r go-analysis-down}
ego_M3_down <- enrichGO(
  gene = res_WT_vs_M3[res_WT_vs_M3$up_down == "down",]$GeneID,
  universe = res_WT_vs_M3$GeneID,
  OrgDb = org.Mm.eg.db,
  keyType = "ENSEMBL",
  ont = "ALL",
  pAdjustMethod = "BH",
  pvalueCutoff = 0.05,
  qvalueCutoff = 0.05,
  readable = TRUE
)
```


```{r}
ego_down_plot <- plot_go_enrichment(ego_M3_down, "downregulated", n_terms = 10)

save_thesis_plot(
  plot = ego_down_plot,
  filename = "/Users/tinkienoob/Master_thesis/MATR3/Deseq2/GO_deseq2_png/go_downregulated.png",
  width = 9,
  height = 13
)

ego_down_plot
```

:::

# GO terms separately

