```{r}
# cat("\n:::\n")


```

# GO compare Tier
```{r}
tier_gene_list <- rank_M3tx_df %>%
  filter(rank_char %in% c("Tier 1", "Tier 2", "Tier 3")) %>%
  filter(GeneID %in% M3_universe) %>%
  distinct(rank_char, GeneID) %>%
  group_by(rank_char) %>%
  summarise(genes = list(unique(GeneID)), .groups = "drop") %>%
  tibble::deframe()


cc_bp <- compareCluster(
  geneCluster   = tier_gene_list,
  fun           = "enrichGO",
  OrgDb         = org.Mm.eg.db,
  keyType       = "ENSEMBL",
  ont           = "ALL",
  universe      = M3_universe,
  pAdjustMethod = "BH",
  pvalueCutoff  = 0.05,
  qvalueCutoff  = 0.05,
  readable      = TRUE
)

```

```{r}
dotplot_cc_bp <- dotplot(
  cc_bp,
  showCategory = 8
) +
  scale_size_continuous(name = "Number of\nMATR3 Targets") +
  scale_color_continuous(name = "Adjusted\np-value") +
  theme_bw(base_size = 19) +
  theme(
    plot.title       = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 20),
    strip.text       = element_text(size = 18, face = "bold", color = "white"),
    strip.background = element_rect(fill = "grey20"),
    axis.title.x     = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x      = element_text(color = "black", size = 20, face = "bold"),
    axis.text.y      = element_text(color = "black", size = 19),
    legend.title     = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"),
    legend.text      = element_text(size = 15),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    plot.margin      = margin(10, 10, 10, 10)) +
  guides(
    color = guide_colorbar(title = "Adjusted\np-value"),
    size  = guide_legend(title = "Number of\nMATR3 Targets"))


save_thesis_plot(
  plot = dotplot_cc_bp,
  filename = "/Users/tinkienoob/Master_thesis/MATR3/Deseq2/GO_deseq2_png/compareCluster_tiers.png",
  width = 10,
  height = 13)

dotplot_cc_bp
```

# Extracting gene tables from GO enrichment results
```{r}
#################################### Convert enrichGO into gene table
# extract_go_gene_table <- function(ego_obj, direction_label = NULL) {
#   as.data.frame(ego_obj) %>%
#     dplyr::select(ID, Description, ONTOLOGY, p.adjust, Count, geneID) %>%
#     separate_rows(geneID, sep = "/") %>%
#     rename(gene_name = geneID) %>%
#     mutate(
#       regulate_dir = direction_label,
#       gene_symbol = str_trim(gene_name)
#     ) %>%
#     distinct()
# }
# 
# ego_up_long   <- extract_go_gene_table(ego_M3_up,   "Up")
# ego_down_long <- extract_go_gene_table(ego_M3_down, "Down")
# 
# ego_all_long <- bind_rows(ego_up_long, ego_down_long)
```


```{r}
################################### Terms of interest
# terms_of_interest <- c(
#   "heart development",
#   "cardiac muscle tissue development",
#   "heart morphogenesis",
#   "cardiac chamber development",
#   "heart contraction",
#   "cardiac muscle contraction",
#   "regulation of cardiac muscle contraction",
#   "sarcomere organization",
#   "myofibril assembly",
#   "myofibril organization",
#   "actin filament organization",
#   "actin cytoskeleton organization"
# )
# 
# selected_go_df <- ego_all_long %>%
#  # filter(p.adjust < 0.05) %>%
#   mutate(
#     theme = case_when(
#       grepl(
#         "heart development|heart morphogenesis|cardiac chamber development|cardiac muscle tissue development|cardiac cell development|heart looping",
#         tolower(Description)
#       ) ~ "Cardiac development",
# 
#       grepl(
#         "heart contraction|cardiac muscle contraction|regulation of cardiac muscle contraction|regulation of heart contraction",
#         tolower(Description)
#       ) &
#       !grepl("smooth muscle", tolower(Description)) ~ "Cardiac contraction",
# 
#       grepl(
#         "sarcomere|myofibril|contractile fiber",
#         tolower(Description)
#       ) ~ "Sarcomere / myofibril",
# 
#       grepl(
#         "actin filament|actin cytoskeleton",
#         tolower(Description)
#       ) ~ "Actin cytoskeleton",
# 
#       TRUE ~ NA_character_
#     )
#   ) %>%
#   filter(!is.na(theme))
```


```{r}
# ################################### Join tier info and count genes
# ego_cardiac_theme_df <- selected_go_df %>%
#   left_join(
#     rank_M3tx_df %>% dplyr::select(gene_id, gene_name, rank),
#     by = "gene_name"
#   ) %>%
#   filter(!is.na(rank)) %>%
#   distinct(regulate_dir, theme, gene_name, rank)
# 
# ego_theme_cardiac_counts <- ego_cardiac_theme_df %>%
#   dplyr::count(regulate_dir, theme, rank, name = "n_genes") %>%
#   complete(
#     regulate_dir,
#     theme = c(
#       "Cardiac development",
#       "Cardiac contraction",
#       "Sarcomere / myofibril",
#       "Actin cytoskeleton"
#     ),
#     rank = c("Tier1", "Tier2", "Tier3"),
#     fill = list(n_genes = 0)
#   )
# 
# ggplot(ego_theme_cardiac_counts, aes(x = n_genes, y = theme, fill = rank)) +
#   geom_col(position = position_dodge(width = 0.75), width = 0.7) +
#   facet_wrap(~ regulate_dir) +
#   scale_fill_manual(values = tier_colors) +
#   labs(
#     x = "Number of genes",
#     y = NULL,
#     fill = "Tier",
#     title = "Tier distribution across cardiac-relating functional themes"
#   ) +
#   theme_bw(base_size = 12)
```


```{r}
ego_t1 <- enrichGO(
  gene = tier_gene_list$`Tier 1`,
  universe = M3_universe,
  OrgDb = org.Mm.eg.db,
  keyType = "ENSEMBL",
  ont = "ALL",
  pAdjustMethod = "BH",
  pvalueCutoff = 0.05,
  qvalueCutoff = 0.05,
  readable = TRUE
)
  
ego_t1_simplified <- simplify(
  ego_t1,
  cutoff = 0.7,
  by = "p.adjust",
  select_fun = min
)

table(rank_M3tx_df$rank_char)
```

# GO tier 1
```{r}
go_long <- as.data.frame(ego_t1_simplified) %>%
  separate(GeneRatio, into = c("gene_count", "bg_count"), sep = "/", convert = TRUE) %>%
  mutate(
    GeneRatio_num = gene_count / bg_count
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(ID, Description, Count, p.adjust, GeneRatio_num, geneID) %>%
  separate_rows(geneID, sep = "/") %>%
  rename(gene_symbol = geneID) %>%
  left_join(
    res_WT_vs_M3 %>%
      dplyr::select(gene_name, up_down),
    by = c("gene_symbol" = "gene_name")
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    regulation = case_when(
      up_down == "up"   ~ "Upregulated",
      up_down == "down" ~ "Downregulated",
      TRUE              ~ "Not regulated"
    )
  )

# -----------------------------
# 3. Count genes per term and direction
# -----------------------------
go_plot_df <- go_long %>%
  group_by(ID, Description, Count, p.adjust, GeneRatio_num, regulation) %>%
  summarise(n = n(), .groups = "drop")

# -----------------------------
# 4. Pick top terms
# -----------------------------
top_terms <- go_plot_df %>%
  distinct(ID, Description, Count, p.adjust, GeneRatio_num) %>%
  arrange(desc(GeneRatio_num), p.adjust) %>%
  slice_head(n = 12) %>%
  mutate(term_label = str_wrap(Description, width = 40))

go_plot_df_top <- go_plot_df %>%
  semi_join(top_terms, by = "ID") %>%
  left_join(
    top_terms %>% dplyr::select(ID, term_label),
    by = "ID"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    term_label = fct_reorder(term_label, GeneRatio_num),
    regulation = factor(
      regulation,
      levels = c("Upregulated", "Downregulated", "Not regulated")
    )
  )

p_tier1_stack3 <- ggplot(
  go_plot_df_top,
  aes(x = n, y = term_label, fill = regulation)
) +
  geom_col(width = 0.6) +   # thinner bars

  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      "Upregulated"   = "#9FBAD6",
      "Downregulated" = "#08306B",
      "Not regulated" = "grey80"
    )
  ) +

  labs(
    x = "Number of genes in GO term",
    y = NULL
  ) +

  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 13),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.title.x = element_text(face = "bold"),
    
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    
    legend.position = "right",
    legend.title = element_blank()
  )

print(p_tier1_stack3)

save_thesis_plot(
  plot = p_tier1_stack3,
  filename = "/Users/tinkienoob/Master_thesis/MATR3/Deseq2/GO_deseq2_png/tier1_enrichment.png",
  width = 8,
  height = 7
)
```


```{r}
# ── Tier 1 downregulated genes ─────────────────
tier1_down <- MAplot_tier_M3vsWT %>%
  filter(rank == "High", up_down == "down") %>%
  filter(!is.na(gene_id), !is.na(log2FoldChange.ashr))

# ── GO gene mapping ────────────────────────────
go_gene_tier1 <- ego_t1_simplified@result %>%
  dplyr::select(ID, Description, geneID) %>%
  separate_rows(geneID, sep = "/") %>%
  rename(gene_id = geneID)

# ── JOIN (FIXED) ───────────────────────────────
go_gene_tier1_df <- go_gene_tier1 %>%
  left_join(
    tier1_down %>% dplyr::select(gene_name, log2FoldChange.ashr),
    by = c("gene_id" = "gene_name")
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(log2FoldChange.ashr))

term_order <- go_plot_df_top %>%
  distinct(term_label) %>%
  pull(term_label)

go_gene_tier1_df <- go_gene_tier1_df %>%
  mutate(
    Description = factor(Description, levels = rev(term_order))
  )

library(ggrepel)

p_fc <- ggplot(
  go_gene_tier1_df,
  aes(x = Description, y = log2FoldChange.ashr)
) +
  
  geom_boxplot(
    fill = "grey90",
    width = 0.4,
    outlier.shape = NA
  ) +
  
  geom_jitter(
    width = 0.15,
    size = 1.8,
    color = "#2C7FB8",
    alpha = 0.7
  ) +

  # 🔥 LABEL ONLY EXTREME GENES
  geom_text_repel(
    data = go_gene_tier1_df %>%
      group_by(Description) %>%
      slice_min(log2FoldChange.ashr, n = 1) %>%   # most downregulated
      bind_rows(
        go_gene_tier1_df %>%
          group_by(Description) %>%
          slice_max(log2FoldChange.ashr, n = 1)   # most upregulated
      ),
    aes(label = gene_id),
    size = 5,
    box.padding = 0.3,
    point.padding = 0.2,
    segment.color = "grey70",
    max.overlaps = 50
  ) +
  
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "black", width = 1) +
  
  coord_flip() +
  
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = "Log2 Fold Change"
  ) +
  
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    axis.text.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank()
  )

library(patchwork)

p_combined <- p_fc + p_tier1_stack3 +
  plot_layout(widths = c(1, 1.2))

ggsave(
  filename = "/Users/tinkienoob/Master_thesis/MATR3/Tier_targets_png/tier1_GO_combined.png",
  plot = p_combined,
  width = 14,   # 🔥 increase width
  height = 7,
  dpi = 300
)
```


## Heatmap
This heatmap shows gene expression patterns across all samples (with rows representing genes and columns representing samples) to visualize co-regulated genes or differential expressed genes.
Z-score is values representing how far a data point is from the mean, in units of standard deviation (sds - variation in gene expression). The result later can be used for clustering relative expression genes. 

### Normalization:
The count data of a particular generepresenting the number of sequence reads that reflects both gene expression and various technical factors. The higher the number of counts, the more reads associated with that gene, and the assumption that there was a higher level of expression of that gene in the sample.
The counts of mapped reads for each gene is proportional to the expression of RNA (“interesting”) in addition to many other factors (“uninteresting”). Normalization is the process of scaling raw count values to account for the “uninteresting” factors. Normalization is essential to correct for these biases (e.g., sequencing depth, gene length, RNA composition), ensuring the expression levels are more comparable between and within samples and accurately reflect biological differences.

Key factors addressed during normalization:
- Sequencing depth: Adjusts for varying numbers of reads per sample.
- Gene length: Important for transcript-level quantification.
- RNA composition: Accounts for highly expressed genes skewing the distribution.
https://hbctraining.github.io/Training-modules/planning_successful_rnaseq/lessons/sample_level_QC.html 
```{r}
dds_matrix <- as.matrix(dds_count_df)

rownames(dds_matrix) <- dds_matrix[,1]
dds_matrix <- dds_matrix[,-1]
mode(dds_matrix) <- "numeric"

# Z-score normalization per gene (row)
# Remove genes with all-zero counts
matrix_nozero <- dds_matrix[
  apply(dds_matrix, 1, function(x) any(x != 0)),]

# calculate sd. and mean for each row
row_means <- rowMeans(matrix_nozero, na.rm = TRUE)
row_sds   <- apply(matrix_nozero, 1, sd, na.rm = TRUE)

z_matrix <- sweep(matrix_nozero, 1, row_means, "-") # using sweep to subtract row means
z_matrix <- sweep(z_matrix, 1, row_sds, "/") # 1 is to apply this operation to each row 

# replace NA value with 0 (genes with constant expression across samples will have sd = 0, leading to NA after division)
z_matrix[is.na(z_matrix)] <- 0
```

Now we have a matrix (matrix_nozero) containing z-scores. Z-score normalization per gene (row), which standardizes the expression across samples (each row will have a mean ~0 and std ~1 (except for rows with constant values)). Z-score is a form of standardization, but not a normalization method for RNA-seq counts.
What you did:
For each gene, you subtract the mean and divide by the standard deviation across samples.
This gives relative up/down patterns per gene (red = higher than average, blue = lower).
Purpose: visualize patterns/clustering, not absolute expression levels.

### K-means clustering calculation
The first step when using k-means clustering is to indicate the number of clusters (k) that will be generated in the final solution. The algorithm starts by randomly selecting k objects from the data set to serve as the initial centers for the clusters. The selected objects are also known as cluster means or centroids. Next, each of the remaining objects is assigned to it’s closest centroid, where closest is defined using the Euclidean distance (Eq. 1) between the object and the cluster mean. This step is called “cluster assignment step”. After the assignment step, the algorithm computes the new mean value of each cluster. The term cluster “centroid update” is used to design this step. Now that the centers have been recalculated, every observation is checked again to see if it might be closer to a different cluster. All the objects are reassigned again using the updated cluster means. The cluster assignment and centroid update steps are iteratively repeated until the cluster assignments stop changing (i.e until convergence is achieved). That is, the clusters formed in the current iteration are the same as those obtained in the previous iteration.(with nstart is a number of random initial configurations (random sets of cluster centroids) the algorithm will try.)
https://uc-r.github.io/kmeans_clustering 
```{r}
# If mat is a data frame, convert and scale if needed
z_sample <- z_matrix[sample(nrow(z_matrix), 10000), ]

# Run the elbow method on the sample
fviz_nbclust(
  z_sample,
  kmeans,
  method = "wss",
  k.max = 10,
  nstart = 25 # Improve stability
) +
labs(title = "Elbow Method for Optimal k on random 10k genes")
```
### Heatmap annotation
```{r}
coLor = colorRamp2(c(-1, 0, 1), c("dodgerblue3", "white", "firebrick3"))

regulation_colors <- c(
    "up" = "darkorange",
    "down" = "#009E73",
    "not regulated" = "white"
  )

# Genes of interest
  genes_of_interest <- c(
  "Rbm20","Matr3","Xist","Ttn","Cacna1a","Cacna1b","Cacna1c","Cacna1d",
  "Ldb3","Pdlim5","Camk2a","Camk2d","Camk2g","Rbm14","Rbm24","Rbm39",
  "Rbm8a","Rbpms","Rbpms2","Mbnl1","Mbnl2","Nppa","Nppb","Myh6","Myh7",
  "Hnrnpu","Srsf3","Tnnt2","Des","Acta1","Actn2","Mybpc1","Mybpc3","Myocd"
)
```

### Column cluster by genes
```{r}
set.seed(123)
# column cluster by different replicates/ conditions
columncluster = kmeans(t(z_matrix), centers = 2, nstart = 25)
columnclustersort = order(columncluster$cluster)
```


### Heatmap with different kmeans to identify best clustering
```{r}
cluster_list <- list()

for (k in 5:8) {

  set.seed(123)

  # Run k-means on rows
  km <- kmeans(z_matrix, centers = k, nstart = 25)

  row_split <- km$cluster
  ord <- order(row_split)

  cluster_list[[paste0("k", k)]] <- row_split

  # Reorder matrix
  z_ordered <- z_matrix[ord, columnclustersort]
  row_split_ordered <- row_split[ord]
  
  # counts per cluster
  cluster_levels = sort(unique(row_split_ordered))

  # Get high-confidence genes
  is_highconf <- rownames(z_ordered) %in% rank_M3tx_df$gene_id
  
  # Count high-confidence genes per cluster
  highconf_counts <- sapply(cluster_levels, function(cluster) {
    sum(is_highconf[row_split_ordered == cluster])
  })
  
  # Map the cluster-wise counts to each row (gene) in the matrix
  count_bar_values = highconf_counts[match(row_split_ordered, cluster_levels)]

  # Show label only once per cluster
  clustercount_labels = ifelse(!duplicated(row_split_ordered), count_bar_values, "")
  
  # L2FC annotation: 
  matr3_anno <- rowAnnotation(
  Lfc_Matr3 = res_WT_vs_M3$up_down[match(rownames(z_ordered), res_WT_vs_M3$gene_id)],
  col = list(Lfc_Matr3 = regulation_colors)
)

rbm20_anno <- rowAnnotation(
  Lfc_RBM20 = res_WT_vs_RBM20$up_down[match(rownames(z_ordered), res_WT_vs_RBM20$gene_id)],
  col = list(Lfc_RBM20 = regulation_colors)
)

dko_anno <- rowAnnotation(
  Lfc_dKO = res_WT_vs_dKO$up_down[match(rownames(z_ordered), res_WT_vs_dKO$gene_id)],
  col = list(Lfc_dKO = regulation_colors)
)
  
  ############################ 4. Barplot annotation of number of high confidence genes IN EACH CLUSTER ############################
  ha <- rowAnnotation(
    "highconf\ngene\ncounts" = anno_barplot(
      count_bar_values,
      gp = gpar(col = "#45B7D1", fill = "#45B7D1"),
      bar_width = 0.8,
      width = unit(2, "cm"),
    axis_param = list(
      at = c(0, max(count_bar_values, na.rm = TRUE)),
      labels = c("0", as.character(max(count_bar_values, na.rm = TRUE))),
      gp = gpar(fontsize = 8))),
    bar_label = anno_text(
      clustercount_labels,
      gp = gpar(fontsize = 8),
      just = "left",
      location = 0.1),
    annotation_name_gp = gpar(fontsize = 9, fontface = "bold"), # Set the name of this label column
    annotation_name_rot = 0) # Horizontal text for title

  # Heatmap
  hm <- Heatmap(
    z_ordered,
    name = "z-score",
    column_title = paste0("K-means Clustering (k = ", k, ")"),
    cluster_columns = FALSE,
    cluster_rows = FALSE,
    row_split = row_split_ordered,
    right_annotation = c(matr3_anno, rbm20_anno, dko_anno, ha),
    col = coLor,
    show_row_names = FALSE,
    use_raster = TRUE
  )

  draw(hm)
}
```


# Heatmap of each tier with different k-means clustering
```{r}
tier_heatmap <- function(tier_name, k_clusters, show_labels = FALSE) {
  
  set.seed(123)
  z_tier <- z_matrix[rownames(z_matrix) %in% rank_M3tx_df$gene_id[rank_M3tx_df$rank == tier_name], , drop = FALSE]
  
  # K-means clustering
  row_km <- kmeans(z_tier, centers = k_clusters, nstart = 25, iter.max = 100)
  row_split <- row_km$cluster
  row_order <- order(row_split)
  
  # Order matrix
  z_ordered <- z_tier[row_order, columnclustersort]

  # Annotation matching
  idx_M3   <- match(rownames(z_ordered), res_WT_vs_M3$gene_id)
  idx_RBM  <- match(rownames(z_ordered), res_WT_vs_RBM20$gene_id)
  idx_dKO  <- match(rownames(z_ordered), res_WT_vs_dKO$gene_id)
  
  # Row annotations
  matr3_anno <- rowAnnotation(
    L2fc_Matr3 = res_WT_vs_M3$up_down[idx_M3],
    col = list(L2fc_Matr3 = regulation_colors))
  
  rbm20_anno <- rowAnnotation(
    L2fc_RBM20 = res_WT_vs_RBM20$up_down[idx_RBM],
    col = list(L2fc_RBM20 = regulation_colors))
  
  dko_anno <- rowAnnotation(
    L2fc_dKO = res_WT_vs_dKO$up_down[idx_dKO],
    col = list(L2fc_dKO = regulation_colors))
 right_anno <- c(matr3_anno, rbm20_anno, dko_anno)
  
  if (show_labels) {
    
    gene_symbols <- res_WT_vs_M3$gene_name[idx_M3]
    goi_indices <- which(!is.na(gene_symbols) & gene_symbols %in% genes_of_interest)
    
    if (length(goi_indices) > 0) {
      
      symbol_anno <- rowAnnotation(
        gene_symbol = anno_mark(
          at = goi_indices,
          labels = gene_symbols[goi_indices],
          labels_gp = gpar(fontsize = 8, fontface = "bold")))
      
      right_anno <- c(right_anno, symbol_anno)}
  }
  
  # Heatmap
  hm <- Heatmap(
    z_ordered,
    name = "z-score of normalized counts",
    col = coLor,
    cluster_rows = FALSE,
    cluster_columns = TRUE,
    row_split = row_split[row_order],
    column_title = paste0(tier_name, " set of genes"),
    show_row_names = FALSE,
    right_annotation = right_anno,
    use_raster = TRUE
  )
  
  draw(hm)
}

tier_heatmap("Tier1", k_clusters = 4, show_labels = TRUE)
tier_heatmap("Tier2", k_clusters = 6, show_labels = TRUE)
tier_heatmap("Tier3", k_clusters = 6, show_labels = TRUE)

```



# Classification of compartments: 
https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38964322/
```{r}
library(readxl)

compartments_path <- '/Users/tinkienoob/Master_thesis/MATR3/m3_paper/Genome-wide quantification of RNA flow across subcellular compartments reveals determinants of the mammalian transcript life cycle/mmc2.xlsx'

# 2. Define the sheets you want to read
compartment_sheets <- c("3T3_rates_rep1", "3T3_rates_rep2")

# 3. Read them into a list using lapply
compartments_list <- lapply(compartment_sheets, function(sheet) {
  read_excel(compartments_path, sheet = sheet)
})

# 4. Assign names to the list elements for easy access
names(compartments_list) <- compartment_sheets

compartment_rate_rep1 <- compartments_list[["3T3_rates_rep1"]]
compartment_rate_rep2 <- compartments_list[["3T3_rates_rep2"]]

compartment_rate_merged <- compartment_rate_rep1 %>%
  inner_join(compartment_rate_rep2, by="Gene", suffix=c("_r1","_r2"))

```


```{r}
# Get Matrin3 high confidence transcrips rates
Matrin3_rates <- compartment_rate_merged %>%
  filter(Gene %in% rank_M3tx_df$GeneID) %>%
  dplyr::select(Gene, ends_with(c("Mean_r1", "Mean_r2", "MAP_r1", "MAP_r2"))) 

Matrin3_rates_mean <- Matrin3_rates %>%
   mutate(
    across(matches(c("Mean_r[12]$", "MAP_r[12]$")),
           ~ suppressWarnings(as.numeric(.)))
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    chr_mean = rowMeans(dplyr::select(., matches("half_life_chr.Mean_r[12]$")), na.rm = TRUE),
    nuc_mean = rowMeans(dplyr::select(., matches("half_life_nuc.Mean_r[12]$")), na.rm = TRUE),
    nucexp_mean = rowMeans(dplyr::select(., matches("half_life_nucexp_from_nucres_r[12]$")), na.rm = TRUE),
    cyto_mean = rowMeans(dplyr::select(., matches("half_life_cyto.Mean_r[12]$")), na.rm = TRUE),
    poly_entry_mean = rowMeans(dplyr::select(., matches("half_life_poly_entry.Mean_r[12]$")), na.rm = TRUE),
    chr_release_meanMAP = rowMeans(dplyr::select(., matches("half_life_chr_release_from_nucdeg.MAP_r[12]$")), na.rm = TRUE),
    nudegr_meanMAP = rowMeans(dplyr::select(., matches("half_life_nucdeg.MAP_r[12]$")), na.rm = TRUE),
    whole_mean = rowMeans(dplyr::select(., matches("half_life_whole_cell.Mean_r[12]$")), na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  left_join(rank_M3tx_df %>% 
              dplyr::select(GeneID, rank),
            by = c("Gene" = "GeneID"))

Matrin3_long <- Matrin3_rates_mean %>%
  dplyr::select(Gene, rank,
         chr_mean,
         nuc_mean,
         cyto_mean,
         nucexp_mean,
         poly_entry_mean,
         chr_release_meanMAP,
         nudegr_meanMAP,
         whole_mean) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = -c(Gene, rank),
    names_to = "Stage",
    values_to = "Half_life") %>%
  mutate(
    Stage = recode(Stage,
                   chr_mean = "Chromatin",
                   nuc_mean = "Nuclear",
                   cyto_mean = "Cytoplasm",
                   nucexp_mean = "Nuclear export",
                   poly_entry_mean = "Untranslated cytoplasm",
                   chr_release_meanMAP = "Chromatin release",
                   nudegr_meanMAP = "Nuclear degradation",
                   whole_mean = "Whole Cell")) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Half_life))
```


Nucleus → Cytoplasm → (Untranslated) → Polysome → Decay
Chromatin
Nuclear
Cytoplasm
```{r}
stage_counts <- Matrin3_long %>%
  group_by(Stage, rank) %>%
  summarise(n = n(), .groups = "drop")

ggplot(Matrin3_long,
       aes(x = rank,
           y = Half_life,
           fill = rank)) +
  
  geom_violin(trim = FALSE, width = 0.7, alpha = 0.6, color = NA) +
  
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.3, alpha = 0.3,
               outlier.shape = NA, color = "black") +
  
  geom_text(data = stage_counts,
            aes(x = rank,
                y = 1200,
                label = paste0("n=", n)),
            size = 3.5,
            vjust = -0.5,
            inherit.aes = FALSE) +
  
  facet_wrap(~ Stage, scales = "fixed") +
  
  scale_y_continuous(
    trans = "log2",
    limits = c(1, 1800),
    breaks = c(1, 4, 16, 64, 256, 1024)) +
  
  scale_fill_manual(values = tier_colors) +
  
  labs(
    x = "",
    y = "Half-life (minutes)") +
  
  theme_bw(base_size = 15) +
  theme_thesis() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    legend.position = "none")
```

```{r}
ks_results <- Matrin3_long %>%
  group_by(Stage) %>%
  group_split() %>%
  map_df(function(df) {
    pairs <- combn(unique(df$rank), 2, simplify = FALSE)
    
    map_df(pairs, function(p) {
      x <- df %>% filter(rank == p[1]) %>% pull(Half_life)
      y <- df %>% filter(rank == p[2]) %>% pull(Half_life)
      
      ks <- ks.test(x, y, exact = FALSE)
      
      data.frame(
        Stage = unique(df$Stage),
        group1 = p[1],
        group2 = p[2],
        p_value = ks$p.value
      )
    })
  }) %>%
  mutate(
    p_adj = p.adjust(p_value, method = "BH"),
    signif_label = case_when(
      p_adj < 0.001 ~ "***",
      p_adj < 0.01  ~ "**",
      p_adj < 0.05  ~ "*",
      TRUE          ~ "ns"),
    label = paste0(group2, "-", group1, ": ", signif_label)) 
  
  
ks_results <- ks_results %>%
  group_by(Stage) %>%
  mutate(
    y_pos = seq(0.9, 0.7, length.out = n())
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

```

```{r}
a <- ggplot(Matrin3_long,
       aes(x = Half_life, color = rank)) +
  stat_ecdf(
    geom = "point",
    aes(y = after_stat(y)),
  #  alpha = 0.2,
    size = 0.6,
    position = "identity") +
  # geom_text(
  #   data = ks_results,
  #   aes(x = Inf, y = y_pos, label = label), # x = Inf pushes to the right edge
  #   hjust = 2.59,                           # Right-aligns text with a small margin
  #   vjust = 0.5,                              # Bottom-aligns the text box
  #   inherit.aes = FALSE, 
  #   size = 5
  # ) +
  facet_wrap(~ Stage, scales = "free_x", ncol = 2) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    trans = "log2",
    breaks = c(1, 4, 16, 64, 256, 1024),
    labels = c(1, 4, 16, 64, 256, 1024)) +
  scale_color_manual(values = tier_colors) +
  labs(
    title = "",
    x = "Half-life (minutes)",
    y = "Cumulative probability",
    color = "Rank") +
  theme_bw(base_size = 16) +
  theme_thesis() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"))

a
save_thesis_plot(
  plot = a,
  filename = "/Users/tinkienoob/Master_thesis/MATR3/Deseq2/GO_deseq2_png/half_life_ecdf.png",
  width = 10,
  height = 12
)
```
ECDF analysis of mRNA half-lives across subcellular compartments showed that Tier1, Tier2, and Tier3 MATR3 targets largely shared similar half-life distributions in chromatin, nuclear, cytoplasmic, untranslated cytoplasmic, and whole-cell fractions. Only modest separation was observed for chromatin-release estimates, where Tier1 targets appeared slightly left-shifted relative to Tier3, suggesting somewhat faster release dynamics. Overall, these results indicate that MATR3 target confidence is not associated with major global differences in transcript stability, supporting a primary role for MATR3 in RNA processing rather than broad regulation of mRNA decay.
he transcripts in Tier1 tend to have somewhat shorter estimated half-lives for the chromatin-release step than Tier3. In practical terms, those RNAs may spend less time in the chromatin-associated state before moving onward.

That could happen for several reasons:
	•	these transcripts are inherently processed more efficiently
	•	they are a transcript class with distinct gene architecture => because more introns? or else? 
	•	MATR3 binds them during an early processing window => How to know? 
	•	the estimate reflects a composite of transcription, splicing, and release, not a single pure mechanism
	


```{r}
ggplot(Matrin3_long,
       aes(x = Half_life, color = rank)) +
  stat_ecdf(
    geom = "point",
    aes(y = after_stat(y)),
    size = 0.6,
    position = "identity") +
  
  # 🔥 UPDATED TEXT ALIGNMENT 🔥
  geom_text(
    data = ks_results,
    aes(x = -Inf, y = y_pos, label = label), # x = -Inf anchors to the LEFT edge
    hjust = 1.05,                            # Left-aligns text, small padding from axis
    vjust = 0.5,                             
    inherit.aes = FALSE, 
    size = 5
  ) +
  
  facet_wrap(~ Stage, scales = "free_x", ncol = 2) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    trans = "log2",
    breaks = c(1, 4, 16, 64, 256, 1024),
    labels = c(1, 4, 16, 64, 256, 1024)) +
  scale_color_manual(values = tier_colors) +
  labs(
    title = "",
    x = "Half-life (minutes)",
    y = "Cumulative probability",
    color = "Rank") +
  theme_bw(base_size = 16) +
  theme_thesis() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"))
```


# Alluvial diagram
```{r}
library(ggalluvial)
get_regulation <- function(df, condition_label) {
  df %>%
    dplyr::transmute(
      GeneID,
      gene_id,
      gene_name,
      regulation = dplyr::case_when(
        !is.na(padj) & padj < 0.05 & log2FoldChange.ashr > 0 ~ "Up",
        !is.na(padj) & padj < 0.05 & log2FoldChange.ashr < 0 ~ "Down",
        TRUE ~ "Not regulated"
      )
    ) %>%
    dplyr::rename(!!condition_label := regulation)
}

tier_alluvial_df <- rank_M3tx_df %>%
  dplyr::filter(rank %in% c("Tier1", "Tier2", "Tier3")) %>%
  dplyr::distinct(GeneID, gene_id, gene_name, rank) %>%
  dplyr::left_join(
    get_regulation(res_WT_vs_M3, "Matr3_cKO"),
    by = c("GeneID", "gene_id", "gene_name")
  ) %>%
  dplyr::left_join(
    get_regulation(res_WT_vs_RBM20, "RBM20_KO"),
    by = c("GeneID", "gene_id", "gene_name")
  ) %>%
  dplyr::left_join(
    get_regulation(res_WT_vs_dKO, "dKO"),
    by = c("GeneID", "gene_id", "gene_name")
  ) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    rank      = factor(rank, levels = c("Tier1", "Tier2", "Tier3")),
    Matr3_cKO = factor(Matr3_cKO, levels = c("Up", "Down", "Not regulated")),
    dKO       = factor(dKO,       levels = c("Up", "Down", "Not regulated")),
    RBM20_KO  = factor(RBM20_KO,  levels = c("Up", "Down", "Not regulated"))
  )

tier_alluvial_summary <- tier_alluvial_df %>%
  dplyr::count(rank, Matr3_cKO, dKO, RBM20_KO, name = "n")

############################
# 5. aggregated alluvial plot
############################
p_alluvial_summary <- ggplot(tier_alluvial_summary,
  aes(
    axis1 = rank,
    axis2 = Matr3_cKO,
    axis3 = dKO,
    axis4 = RBM20_KO,
    y = n)) +
  geom_alluvium(
    aes(fill = rank),
    width = 1/3,
    alpha = 0.75) +
  geom_stratum(
    color = "black",
    width = 0.3,
    alpha = 0.15) +
  geom_text(
  stat = "stratum",
  aes(
    label = ifelse(
      after_stat(count) >= 200,
      paste0(as.character(after_stat(stratum)), "\n", after_stat(count)),
      "")),
  size = 3.8,
  lineheight = 0.9,
  fontface = "bold") +
  scale_x_discrete(
  limits = c("Tier", "Matr3-cKO", "dKO", "RBM20-KO")) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = tier_colors) +
  labs(
    y = "Gene Count",
    x = "",
    title = "Alluvial Diagram of Tier Genes Across KO Conditions") +
   theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12))

p_alluvial_summary

save_thesis_plot(
  plot = p_alluvial_summary,
  filename = "/Users/tinkienoob/Master_thesis/MATR3/Deseq2/GO_deseq2_png/alluvial_summary.png",
  width = 10,
  height = 9
)
```

```{r}
down_genes <- go_long %>%
  filter(regulation == "Downregulated") %>%
  distinct(gene_symbol)

down_fc <- down_genes %>%
  left_join(
    res_WT_vs_M3 %>%
      dplyr::select(gene_name, log2FoldChange),
    by = c("gene_symbol" = "gene_name")
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(log2FoldChange))

down_fc_cardiac <- go_long %>%
  filter(
    regulation == "Downregulated",
    str_detect(Description,
      regex("cardiac|heart|muscle|sarcomere", ignore_case = TRUE)
    )
  ) %>%
  distinct(gene_symbol) %>%
  left_join(
    res_WT_vs_M3 %>%
      dplyr::select(gene_name, log2FoldChange),
    by = c("gene_symbol" = "gene_name")
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(log2FoldChange))

p_hist2 <- ggplot(down_fc_cardiac, aes(x = log2FoldChange)) +
  geom_histogram(aes(y = ..density..),
                 bins = 30,
                 fill = "#08306B",
                 alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_density(color = "black", size = 1) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
  theme_bw()
```
